Title: A gene cluster in Ginkgo biloba encodes unique multifunctional cytochrome P450s that initiate ginkgolide biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to levopimaradiene, the proposed diterpene precursor of ginkgolides.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ginkgo biloba; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: WGCNA co-expression analysis using 9 Ginkgo biloba transcriptome datasets from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; genome mining from Ginkgo biloba genome draft; cDNA sequences deposited in GenBank ON759313-ON759329
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in roots; reported as exclusively expressed in roots and absent from leaves and stems; co-expression module also high in seedlings, fibrous roots, and mature fruits
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid (native); retargeted to cytosol in tobacco engineering
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical/nutraceutical activities including neuromodulation, increased cerebral blood flow and circulation, neurotransmission modification, neuroprotection, anti-inflammatory activity, PAF and GABAA receptor antagonism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: entire plant; biosynthesis hypothesized in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces levopimaradiene, the precursor entering the characterized early ginkgolide pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; co-expression analysis; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae; GC-MS/LC-HRMS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GbCYP7005C1; GbCYP7005C3; GbCYP7005C2; GbCYP867E38; GbCYP867K1; GbCYP720B31
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point gene for reconstructing ginkgolide pathway in heterologous hosts; should be stacked with downstream CYPs for product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LPS produces a mixture of up to six diterpene hydrocarbons, with levopimaradiene as the main product.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbLPS is genomically clustered with five CYP genes in a putative ginkgolide biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. It produces levopimaradiene, identified as the precursor utilized by downstream CYP7005C1/C3. 3. Its expression pattern is root-associated and co-expressed with ginkgolide-pathway CYPs.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative scaffold rearrangement toward ginkgosinoic acid A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multifunctional oxidation of levopimaradiene to 2α-hydroxylevopimaradiene and then to ginkgosinoic acid A, involving double C-C bond cleavage and formation of the characteristic tert-butyl group.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: WGCNA co-expression analysis using 9 Ginkgo biloba transcriptome datasets from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; cDNA sequences deposited in GenBank ON759313-ON759329
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GbLPS; high mainly in seedlings, fibrous roots, and mature fruits; absent from leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane (inferred for plant CYP)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical/nutraceutical activities including neuromodulation, neuroprotection, increased cerebral blood flow, anti-inflammatory activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: entire plant; pathway proposed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates oxidative rearrangement from levopimaradiene to ginkgosinoic acid A, generating the tert-butyl moiety and basis for one lactone ring.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; stable yeast expression; metabolite profiling by LC-HRMS; NMR structural elucidation; substrate replacement and feeding assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downstream products are reactive/unstable in heterologous hosts, yielding glycosylated or glutathione-conjugated derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential enzyme for pathway reconstruction and metabolic engineering of ginkgolide precursors; should be paired with GbCYP7005C3 only if redundancy desired and with POR2 for improved activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with GbCYP7005C3; only uses levopimaradiene, not dehydroabietadiene-rich products from Coleus forskohlii TPSs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP7005C1 converts levopimaradiene into ginkgosinoic acid A via unusual scarless C-C cleavage chemistry. 2. It also forms 2α-hydroxylevopimaradiene and 2α-hydroxydehydroabietadiene as intermediate/side products. 3. Together with GbCYP7005C3, it defines the first committed oxidative transformation toward ginkgolides.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative scaffold rearrangement toward ginkgosinoic acid A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multifunctional oxidation of levopimaradiene to 2α-hydroxylevopimaradiene and then to ginkgosinoic acid A, involving double C-C bond cleavage and formation of the characteristic tert-butyl group.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: WGCNA co-expression analysis using 9 Ginkgo biloba transcriptome datasets from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; cDNA sequences deposited in GenBank ON759313-ON759329
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GbLPS; high mainly in seedlings, fibrous roots, and mature fruits; absent from leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane (inferred for plant CYP)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical/nutraceutical activities including neuromodulation, neuroprotection, increased cerebral blood flow, anti-inflammatory activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: entire plant; pathway proposed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redundantly initiates oxidative rearrangement from levopimaradiene to ginkgosinoic acid A.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; stable yeast expression; metabolite profiling by LC-HRMS; NMR structural elucidation; feeding assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downstream products are reactive/unstable in heterologous hosts, yielding glycosylated or glutathione-conjugated derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Redundant alternative to GbCYP7005C1 for ginkgolide precursor engineering; POR2 improved product titer in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows identical catalytic activity to GbCYP7005C1 and shares high sequence identity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP7005C3 mirrors GbCYP7005C1 activity on levopimaradiene. 2. It forms ginkgosinoic acid A plus minor intermediate/side products 8 and 9. 3. It acts redundantly in the early ginkgolide pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of ginkgosinoic acid A to ginkgosinoic acid B
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ginkgosinoic acid A at C12 to form ginkgosinoic acid B.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: WGCNA co-expression analysis using 9 Ginkgo biloba transcriptome datasets from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; cDNA sequences deposited in GenBank ON759313-ON759329
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In GbLPS co-expression module; high mainly in seedlings, fibrous roots, and mature fruits; absent from leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane (inferred for plant CYP)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical/nutraceutical activities including neuromodulation, neuroprotection, increased cerebral blood flow, anti-inflammatory activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: entire plant; pathway proposed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Advances pathway by converting ginkgosinoic acid A to ginkgosinoic acid B.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in tobacco; engineered yeast production; LC-HRMS product shift; NMR structural elucidation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product/reactive downstream intermediates can be glycosylated or glutathionylated in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for pathway extension beyond ginkgosinoic acid A; useful in stepwise pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Member of a gymnosperm/pre-angiosperm CYP family with no previously characterized members noted by the authors.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP867E38 causes complete conversion of ginkgosinoic acid A-derived profile to compound 11. 2. NMR identified compound 11 as ginkgosinoic acid B. 3. The enzyme catalyzes C12 hydroxylation, preparing substrate for the NIH-shift step.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of ginkgosinoic acid B to ginkgosinoic acid C
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: ginkgosinoic acid C (detected as glucoside or glutathione conjugates)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ginkgosinoic acid B and drives carbon skeleton rearrangement via alkyl migration (NIH shift) to generate ginkgosinoic acid C.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: WGCNA co-expression analysis using 9 Ginkgo biloba transcriptome datasets from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; cDNA sequences deposited in GenBank ON759313-ON759329
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In GbLPS co-expression module; high mainly in seedlings, fibrous roots, and mature fruits; absent from leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane (inferred for plant CYP)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical/nutraceutical activities including neuromodulation, neuroprotection, increased cerebral blood flow, anti-inflammatory activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: entire plant; pathway proposed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the unusual NIH-shift rearrangement step from ginkgosinoic acid B to ginkgosinoic acid C.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in tobacco; engineered yeast expression; LC-HRMS; NMR of glucoside/aglycone derivative; in vivo substrate feeding of 11 to tobacco expressing CYP867K1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Products are unstable/reactive and become glycosylated or glutathione-conjugated in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GbCYP867E38; GbCYP7005C1; GbCYP7005C3; GbLPS; GbPOR2; GbCYP720B31
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme to recreate the unique ginkgolide carbon skeleton in synthetic biology platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors state this is a rare biosynthetic NIH-shift involving alkyl migration on a previously unsuspected intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP867K1 converts ginkgosinoic acid B to ginkgosinoic acid C derivatives. 2. The transformation involves aromatic oxidation and carbon skeleton rearrangement with alkyl migration. 3. Feeding experiments directly confirmed 11 as its substrate.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ginkgosinoic acid C at C20 to enable lactone ring formation to ginkgolactone C, and further hydroxylates ginkgolactone C to form ginkgolactone D.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for first lactone ring in characterized segment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: WGCNA co-expression analysis using 9 Ginkgo biloba transcriptome datasets from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource; cDNA sequences deposited in GenBank ON759313-ON759329
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In GbLPS co-expression module; high mainly in seedlings, fibrous roots, and mature fruits; absent from leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane (inferred for plant CYP)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical/nutraceutical activities including neuromodulation, neuroprotection, increased cerebral blood flow, anti-inflammatory activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: entire plant; pathway proposed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms the first lactone ring in the pathway by converting ginkgosinoic acid C to ginkgolactone C and D derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression screening guided by WGCNA; transient expression in tobacco; engineered yeast expression; LC-HRMS; NMR of glucoside/aglycone derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Products are highly reactive and observed as glucosides or glutathione conjugates rather than free aglycones in hosts
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GbCYP867K1; GbCYP867E38; GbCYP7005C1; GbCYP7005C3; GbLPS; GbPOR2
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical downstream enzyme to install lactone functionality during pathway engineering; should be stacked after GbCYP867K1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Encoded outside the biosynthetic gene cluster but within the GbLPS co-expression module; CYP720B49 showed no activity in tested combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP720B31 was the only additional co-expressed CYP that extended the pathway beyond ginkgosinoic acid C derivatives. 2. It enables first lactone-ring formation by C20 hydroxylation, producing ginkgolactone C and D derivatives. 3. It acts outside the BGC but within the same co-expression network.
